VAL-CHECK-001.title: Authenticated check returns correct response shape
VAL-CHECK-001.evidence: 3 tests passed: 'has exactly the correct top-level keys' verifies 6 top-level keys (status, confidence, parsedJob, matchedJob, applications, reasons) with correct types and enums; 'parsedJob contains exactly 5 public fields, no internal fields' verifies only originalUrl/companyName/jobTitle/jobLocation/externalJobId are present and internal fields (normalizedCompany, normalizedTitle, normalizedLocation, primaryCanonicalKey, fallbackCanonicalKey) are stripped; 'does not leak workspaceId or userId' confirms no internal IDs in serialized response.
testNames: response shape > has exactly the correct top-level keys (VAL-CHECK-001)
response shape > parsedJob contains exactly 5 public fields, no internal fields
response shape > does not leak workspaceId or userId

VAL-CHECK-002.title: Primary key match returns already_applied with high confidence
VAL-CHECK-002.evidence: Test 'returns already_applied with high confidence for primary key match (VAL-CHECK-002)' passed: mocked Convex returns primary key match for nutrien job 30186-en_US, asserts status='already_applied', confidence='high', reasons contains 'matched_primary_key', matchedJob non-null with correct id/companyName, applications array has 1 entry with userName 'Jane Doe'.
testNames: authenticated success – all 4 status values > returns 'already_applied' with high confidence for primary key match (VAL-CHECK-002)

VAL-CHECK-003.title: Fallback key match returns possible_duplicate with medium confidence
VAL-CHECK-003.evidence: Test 'returns possible_duplicate with medium confidence for fallback key match (VAL-CHECK-003)' passed: mocked Convex returns fallback key match, asserts status='possible_duplicate', confidence='medium', reasons contains 'matched_fallback_key'.
testNames: authenticated success – all 4 status values > returns 'possible_duplicate' with medium confidence for fallback key match (VAL-CHECK-003)

VAL-CHECK-004.title: New parseable URL returns new with low confidence
VAL-CHECK-004.evidence: Test 'returns new with low confidence for no existing match (VAL-CHECK-004)' passed: mocked Convex returns no match for novel URL, asserts status='new', confidence='low', reasons contains 'no_existing_match', matchedJob=null, applications=[].
testNames: authenticated success – all 4 status values > returns 'new' with low confidence for no existing match (VAL-CHECK-004)

VAL-CHECK-005.title: Unparseable URL returns unparseable with null match
VAL-CHECK-005.evidence: Test 'returns unparseable with low confidence for non-job URL (VAL-CHECK-005)' passed: mocked Convex returns unparseable for example.com, asserts status='unparseable', confidence='low', reasons contains 'insufficient_identity_fields', matchedJob=null, applications=[].
testNames: authenticated success – all 4 status values > returns 'unparseable' with low confidence for non-job URL (VAL-CHECK-005)

VAL-CHECK-006.title: 401 for unauthenticated, 403 for disallowed email, distinct messages
VAL-CHECK-006.evidence: 3 tests passed: 'returns 401 for unauthenticated request' asserts 401 status, body has only 'error' key with non-empty string; 'returns 403 for non-allowlisted email' asserts 403 status, body has only 'error' key with non-empty string; 'returns DISTINCT error messages for 401 vs 403' asserts the two error strings are different so the extension can differentiate auth states.
testNames: auth > returns 401 for unauthenticated request (no session)
auth > returns DISTINCT error messages for 401 vs 403

VAL-CHECK-007.title: Missing, empty, or malformed URL returns 400
VAL-CHECK-007.evidence: 4 tests passed: 'returns 400 for missing body', 'returns 400 for empty object body (missing url field)', 'returns 400 for empty URL', 'returns 400 for malformed URL (not-a-url)'. All return HTTP 400 with { error: string }. Never returns 500.
testNames: input validation > returns 400 for missing body
input validation > returns 400 for empty object body (missing url field)
input validation > returns 400 for empty URL
input validation > returns 400 for malformed URL (not-a-url)

VAL-CHECK-008.title: URL with query parameters and fragments parsed correctly
VAL-CHECK-008.evidence: Test 'URL with query params and fragments parsed correctly (VAL-CHECK-008)' passed: URL with ?utm_source=LinkedIn&ref=abc#section returns 200 with parsedJob.externalJobId='30186-en_US', matching the clean version without query/fragment.
testNames: URL parsing edge cases > URL with query params and fragments parsed correctly (VAL-CHECK-008)

VAL-CHECK-009.title: Very long URL handled gracefully
VAL-CHECK-009.evidence: Test 'returns 200 or 400 for URL >2048 chars, never 500 (VAL-CHECK-009)' passed: URL with 2100+ chars returns either 200 or 400, assertion confirms response.status is never 500.
testNames: very long URL handling > returns 200 or 400 for URL >2048 chars, never 500 (VAL-CHECK-009)

VAL-CHECK-010.title: Invalid JSON body returns 400
VAL-CHECK-010.evidence: 2 tests passed: 'returns 400 for invalid JSON body (VAL-CHECK-010)' sends malformed '{{{' body and asserts 400 with error, never 500; 'returns 400 for raw text body' sends plain text with application/json content-type and asserts 400 with error.
testNames: input validation > returns 400 for invalid JSON body (VAL-CHECK-010)
input validation > returns 400 for raw text body

VAL-CROSS-006.title: Primary key takes precedence over fallback key
VAL-CROSS-006.evidence: Test 'primary key match takes precedence over fallback key match (VAL-CROSS-006)' passed: mocked Convex returns primary key match with already_applied/high, asserts status='already_applied', confidence='high', reasons contains 'matched_primary_key' and does NOT contain 'matched_fallback_key', matchedJob.id='primary-job-id'.
testNames: primary key precedence > primary key match takes precedence over fallback key match (VAL-CROSS-006)

summary: Tested 11 assertions (VAL-CHECK-001 through VAL-CHECK-010 plus VAL-CROSS-006): all 11 passed. 20 underlying vitest tests all passed in 47ms. No frictions or blockers encountered.
